instructions.evidence: intro_hook 6/10 — The first true prose sentence (after metadata/TL;DR blocks) is a passive, clause-heavy scene-setter that buries the tension. It reads like a press release. The article's strongest hook material is already present in the TL;DR and the editorial asides ('Annual screenings are mostly theatre'), but none of it leads the body copy. The rewrite opens with a specific time-bound claim and an implied question ('why does March matter?') that pulls the reader forward.

patch.target: On 19 March 2026, most Dubai residents will have moved beyond the initial motivation of New Year's health goals, but only a minority will have reviewed their progress in light of the UAE's specific lifestyle and screening guidelines.
patch.before: On 19 March 2026, most Dubai residents will have moved beyond the initial motivation of New Year's health goals, but only a minority will have reviewed their progress in light of the UAE's specific lifestyle and screening guidelines.
patch.after: By late March 2026, most Dubai residents have quietly abandoned their January health goals—not because the goals were wrong, but because no one told them March is the checkpoint that actually matters.
patch.rationale: Quality axis "intro_hook" scored 6/10: The first true prose sentence (after metadata/TL;DR blocks) is a passive, clause-heavy scene-setter that buries the tension. It reads like a press release. The article's strongest hook material is already present in the TL;DR and the editorial asides ('Annual screenings are mostly theatre'), but none of it leads the body copy. The rewrite opens with a specific time-bound claim and an implied question ('why does March matter?') that pulls the reader forward.

priorIssues.evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
Entity of type FAQPage has no @id — cross-page interconnection blocked
FAQPage schema has 7 Questions but page shows ~13 FAQ pairs
TL;DR is 61 words (target 40–58)
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Human voice vs committee 5/10 — Mixes sharp human asides with generic marketing prose; editorial notes like 'Cut this line' suggest draft artifacts.
Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — Has some opinionated zingers but surrounded by hedged, safe institutional claims.
Unexpected phrasings 6/10 — Lines like 'annual screenings are mostly theatre' and 'swap tactics, not self-worth' are vivid, but padded by boilerplate.
Point of view 6/10 — Has a clear thesis (quarterly > annual) but drifts into neutral explainer mode in the middle sections.
Quotable to a friend 6/10 — A few genuinely quotable lines ('Miss a target? Treat it like a failed A/B test, not a character flaw') but they're buried.
Intro earns attention 4/10 — Opens with bureaucratic TL;DR stacks and a generic framework pitch before any hook lands.
